mission.title: Install and Configure Rancher / RKE2
mission.description: Rancher is a complete container management platform that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters across various environments. It is ideal for organizations looking to streamline their container orchestration and management processes. Key trade-offs include the need for adequate hardware resources and potential complexity in multi-cluster management.

steps.title: Install the Rancher CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Rancher CLI, which is necessary for interacting with the Rancher server.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.14.1/rancher-darwin-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf rancher-darwin-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv rancher /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Rancher CLI for macOS.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using RKE2 with the specified version and node count.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | sh -

sudo systemctl enable rke2-server

sudo systemctl start rke2-server

```

> Install RKE2 on your server.
> Enable the RKE2 server to start on boot.
> Start the RKE2 server.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

sudo cp /etc/rancher/rke2/rke2.yaml ~/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) ~/.kube/config

```

> Copy the RKE2 kubeconfig to your home directory.
> Change ownership of the kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step verifies that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces

```

> Check the status of the nodes in the cluster.
> Verify that all system pods are running.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes optional configurations such as enabling monitoring and setting up RBAC.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/v2.14.1/manifests/rancher-monitoring.yaml

kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(whoami)

```

> Install monitoring for your cluster.
> Grant cluster admin permissions to your user.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rancher
uninstall.description:
This step removes Rancher and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

kubectl delete namespace cattle-system

sudo systemctl stop rke2-server

sudo systemctl disable rke2-server

```

> Delete the Rancher namespace and all resources within it.
> Stop the RKE2 server.
> Disable RKE2 from starting on boot.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rancher
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and verify the current version. This step includes draining nodes if necessary.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/v2.14.1/manifests/rancher.yaml

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n cattle-system

```

> Check the current nodes and their status.
> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Apply the new Rancher manifest for the upgrade.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that all Rancher pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the kubeconfig is not pointing to the correct certificate authority.

**Fix:**
Ensure the kubeconfig file is correctly set up and points to the right CA file.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image "rancher/rancher:v2.14.1": Error response from daemon: pull access denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the Docker daemon cannot access the Rancher image repository.

**Fix:**
Check your internet connection and ensure that Docker is configured to pull from the correct registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the RKE2 server and ensure it is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no nodes available in cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that there are no available nodes in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Verify that the nodes are up and running using 'kubectl get nodes'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved RBAC configurations, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.10 or earlier, ensure to review the new RBAC policies and adjust your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary:
You should see output similar to: 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION
node1 Ready control-plane 10m v1.30.0
node2 Ready <none> 10m v1.30.0'

prerequisites.description: Ensure your environment meets the minimum hardware and software requirements for Rancher.
